title: diodonta aurea
side_effects: Diodonta aurea can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions in some individuals, which may manifest as skin rashes, itching, or difficulty breathing. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to liver damage, as the plant contains toxic compounds that can be detrimental to liver function. Additionally, improper use of the plant may also cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach pain. In severe cases, diodonta aurea can even interact with other medications, leading to adverse effects such as dizziness, fatigue, and increased heart rate.
